attribution.name: PlayfulProcess
attribution.note: The contribute guide, dogfooded as a grammar.

name: How to Contribute — a Grammar of Ways In
description: The contribute guide as a grammar: every item is one way IN — a thing you can make (your own deck, a fix to a historic deck, a sequence/playlist, a course) by an avenue (the app, GitHub, an AI on the repo, or the recursive.eco MCP). Each item carries its `what` and `how` in metadata, so the Emergence Explorer can group by what you want to contribute OR by the skill/tool — the contribute matrix draws itself. Grammars work through every avenue; a course is an MDX file in the repo, so it's code-only. This guide is itself a grammar you can fork and edit — which is exactly its point.

items.name: Your own deck — In the app

sections.What you'll do: Contribute a community grammar — your own deck (in the app). Example: Clown Town.
sections.Steps: Sign in to recursive.eco, open **Create**, and start a new grammar and add your cards. Save, then publish.

metadata.skill: Git / GitHub — edit a file, open a PR

sections.What you'll do: Contribute a community grammar — your own deck (on github). Example: Clown Town.
sections.Steps: On github.com, add a new `tarot/<your-deck>/grammar.json`. Edit it, then **open a pull request** — the change is reviewed before it goes live.

items.name: Your own deck — An AI on the repo

metadata.skill: An AI assistant pointed at the repo

sections.What you'll do: Contribute a community grammar — your own deck (an ai on the repo). Example: Clown Town.
sections.Steps: Point an AI assistant (Claude.ai, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code) at the repo and ask it to start a new grammar and add your cards. Review its draft and open a PR.
sections.Cost: You pay your own AI provider's usage. See [recursive.eco pricing](https://flow.recursive.eco/pricing).

items.name: Your own deck — The recursive.eco MCP

metadata.skill: Claude Code, or your token in any MCP client
metadata.needs: The MCP connected (Claude — or any MCP client)

sections.What you'll do: Contribute a community grammar — your own deck (the recursive.eco mcp). Example: Clown Town.
sections.Steps: With the recursive.eco MCP connected, just **ask** your assistant to start a new grammar and add your cards — it acts on your library directly, no files to touch.
sections.What you need: The MCP connected (Claude — or any MCP client)
sections.Cost: Free to connect. Generated images, audio and storage are billed — see [recursive.eco pricing](https://flow.recursive.eco/pricing).

items.name: Fix a historic deck — In the app

sections.What you'll do: Contribute a correction to a historic deck (in the app). Example: Visconti-Sforza.
sections.Steps: Sign in to recursive.eco, open **Create**, and open the deck and fix the card. Save, then publish.

items.name: Fix a historic deck — On GitHub

metadata.skill: Git / GitHub — edit a file, open a PR

sections.What you'll do: Contribute a correction to a historic deck (on github). Example: Visconti-Sforza.
sections.Steps: On github.com, edit the deck's `grammar.json`. Edit it, then **open a pull request** — the change is reviewed before it goes live.

items.name: A sequence / playlist — In the app

sections.What you'll do: Contribute a sequence grammar — a karaoke or paired set (in the app). Example: Sing & Sing 2.
sections.Steps: Sign in to recursive.eco, open **Create**, and create a sequence grammar (a karaoke or a paired set). Save, then publish.

metadata.skill: Git / GitHub — edit a file, open a PR

sections.Steps: On github.com, add a sequence `grammar.json`. Edit it, then **open a pull request** — the change is reviewed before it goes live.

metadata.skill: Git / GitHub — edit a file, open a PR

sections.What you'll do: Contribute a written course (MDX in the repo) (on github). Example: this guide.
sections.Steps: On github.com, add a course `.mdx` under `pages/courses/`. Edit it, then **open a pull request** — the change is reviewed before it goes live.

items.name: A course — An AI on the repo

metadata.skill: An AI assistant pointed at the repo

sections.What you'll do: Contribute a written course (MDX in the repo) (an ai on the repo). Example: this guide.
sections.Steps: Point an AI assistant (Claude.ai, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code) at the repo and ask it to draft a new course. Review its draft and open a PR.
sections.Cost: You pay your own AI provider's usage. See [recursive.eco pricing](https://flow.recursive.eco/pricing).
